hypothesis: Incorporating a dynamic, real-time adjustment of the bicarbonate-to-carbonate ratio during electrolysis—guided by an online surrogate model that continuously updates based on instantaneous JCO and UCO2 measurements—will maintain reactor yield above 40 mA cm⁻² across varying inlet capture solution compositions, whereas the static optimal ratio of 1.5 M KHCO₃ to 0.75 M K₂CO₃ identified here will yield performance degradation exceeding 20% when the inlet bicarbonate fraction deviates by more than ±0.5 M from this optimum.
